ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE

LAW AND MOTION 302, MTN TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NUMBERS 5, 6, 9, 12, AND 13, AND ORDERS DEFENDANT TO COMPLY WITHIN 10 DAYS. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION AS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NUMBER 10 ON THE BASIS THAT DEFENSE COUNSEL HAS STATED ON THE RECORD TODAY THAT THE AUDIT MATERIALS DO NOT CONTAIN ANY REFERENCES TO EMPLOYEES. THE COURT DENIES THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: CANDACE YOUNT, CSR #2737, TEL NO. (415) 994-5619; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions; Memorandum
Off Calendar Jan-07-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. RICHARD M. ROGERS, Tel No. (415) 981-9788 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. NA'IL BENJAMIN of the Benjamin Law Group, Tel No. (415) 633-8833 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - The Court required parties to appear. Parties informed the Court that they have resolved the issues regarding responses to interrogatories. Argument presented by counsel. The Court ruled as follows: PLAINTIFF REBECA HERNANDEZ-ARCILLA'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions is granted in part and denied in part. The Court grants the motion as to requests for production numbers 5, 6, 9, 12, and 13, and orders Defendant to comply within 10 days. The Court denies the motion as to request for production number 10 on the basis that defense counsel has stated on the record today that the audit materials do not contain any references to employees. The Court denies the request for sanctions. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Candace Yount, CSR #2737, Tel No. (415) 994-5619 (302/EHG)
